displayTitle: Don't Leave It Worse Than You Found It

beats.narration: Last indication read, paperwork signed — and the part still wears the test. Developer, penetrant, residue. The job isn't done.

payload.title: POSTCLEANING
payload.sub: The test isn't finished until the residue comes off.
payload.panelLabel: PT // PART STILL WEARING THE TEST

beats.narration: Why it can't stay: developer powder is a moisture magnet. A patch holds humidity against steel, and a corrosion cell starts under the residue.

payload.heading: Leftover residue does damage
payload.note: MOISTURE-HOLDING PATCH → CORROSION CELL UNDER THE RESIDUE

beats.narration: It bites in four more ways. Coatings won't grip it. Sulfur and halogens attack hot alloys. Oxygen service can ignite. It ruins the next inspection.

payload.heading: Four more reasons it has to go

beats.narration: Timing and method. Never before the record is complete; then promptly. Match removal to residue, mildest that works — never wire brush or blast.
So return the part inspected, documented, clean, dry, and protected. Postcleaning is quick, cheap, unglamorous — and it is part of the test.

payload.sub: Don't leave the part worse than you found it.
